page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon CPU running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs, which correspond to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, and 864.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the n2-highmem-128 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,775.82 and a Passmark CPU score of 84,295.77. Memory subsystem performance is also strong, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 329.21 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 141.27 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are robust, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 31,209.31 and 109.06 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,579.0.

The server offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale databases and in-memory caching that require substantial memory capacity without the overhead of a GPU. A notable tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes. Additionally, while prompt processing for small and medium LLMs is strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.68 tokens/sec due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis deployments, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is an x86_64 server configured with 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 864.0 GB of RAM. Lacking bundled GPUs and local storage, this instance relies entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers strong multi-core and memory performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 84,295.77 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 329.21 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated by AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets. While single-core speeds and large LLM text generation are average to weak, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and high-throughput web serving. It is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale Redis deployments, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is an x86_64 server featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 864 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-128 is an x86_64 server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 864 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 compute node featuring 864 GB of RAM and 128 dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 864 GB of RAM
Strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or bundled GPUs included
Optimized for large databases, Redis, and software compilation
